# LC_patient_0001.pdf

## 第 1 页

PET/CT report
Examination date: 2025-04-01; Tracer: 18F-FDG; Examination items: PET/CT;
Body scan imaging; Height: 1 60
cm; Weight: 77 kg; Department: Specialized
Respiratory Medicine Outpatient Clinic 2; Medical record number:
Clinical diagnosis: 1. Pulmonary mass, 2. Metastatic squamous cell carcinoma of
lymph nodes
Medical history and examination purpose:
The
patient
underwent
total
thyroidectomy
for
thyroid
cancer
on
March
20,2025.
Cervical
lymph
node
pathology
indicated
metastatic
squamous
cell
carcinoma, P40 (isolately +). CT: Lesion in the anterior segment of the left
upper lobe,
3.5*2.3 cm.
Recently, the patient
reported left
neck swelling
and
pain.
A
PET/CT
scan
was
performed
to
assess
the
overall
systemic
condition.
Patient preparation and examination process:
Fasting, establish an intravenous access, and measure blood glucose level at
5.3 mmol/L. Administer
the tracer ¹⁸F-FDG
intravenously (0.14-0.2
mCi/kg body
weight). The patient should remain seated at rest in a quiet, warm, and dimly lit
room, avoiding all external stimuli. Prior to the examination, the patient should
consume 300-500 ml of milk and void. The patient is then placed supine on the
examination
table
for
trunk
(5
beds)
imaging,
which
lasts
approximately
10
minutes.
findings ：
0 L
Neck: Absence of thyroid
tissue postoperatively, with slight
fluffy shadows observed in the
surgical area and slightly increased 
radioactivity uptake, SUVmax 3.5.
Increased radioactivity uptake was
noted in the soft tissues anterior
to the thyroid cartilage.
SUVmax3.9. The inner wall of the
maxillary sinus cavity was smooth, with 
no soft tissue density shadows
observed. The structures of the
nasopharyngeal, oropharyngeal, and

---

## 第 2 页

A patchy radiolucent shadow with abnormally increased uptake, SUVmax 22.4. A
patchy nodular shadow was observed in the right lower lobe near the vertebral
body, with a maximum cross-sectional area of approximately
1.8*1.0 cm, with increased radioactivity uptake, SUVmax 4.3. A cystic radiolucent shadow measuring
approximately 1.0 cm in diameter was additionally observed in the anterior segment of the left upper
lobe. Slight linear shadows were present in both lungs, with no increased radioactivity uptake. Lymph
nodes with increased radioactivity uptake were noted at the left pulmonary hilum and mediastinum
(regions 5 and 6), the largest measuring approximately 1.1 cm in short diameter, with SUVmax 14.9.
Bilateral pleural thickening was localized. The pericardium appeared smooth, with no signs of
radiation.
I I III III II I
I III
I
I
III

---

## 第 3 页

PET/CT examination
report
Check number
Increased uptake of radionuclide in nodules. No fluid accumulation was
observed in the thoracic cavity. No significant abnormal distribution of
radionuclide was detected in the breast, chest wall, or bilateral axillae.
Abdomen
and
Pelvis:
The
liver,
spleen,
pancreas,
bilateral
adrenal
glands,
and
kidneys
were
normal
in
position,
morphology,
and
size,
with
uniform
density
and
normal
radioactivity
distribution.
Small
amounts
of
radioactive urine retention were observed in the renal calyces and renal
pelvis. A punctate high-density shadow was seen in the gallbladder, with no
increased radioactivity uptake. The gastric wall was smooth, with slightly
increased
radioactivity
uptake
in
the
pyloric
region
(SUVmax
3.6).
Segmental
uptake
was
observed
in
part
of
the
intestine.
The
uterus
was
enlarged, with no increased radioactivity uptake. Low-density shadows were
present in the bilateral adnexal regions, with the larger one located on
the
left
side
(approximately
2.7*2.1
cm),
showing
slightly
increased
marginal
radioactivity
uptake
(SUVmax
2.5).
No
definite
enlargement
or
increased radioactivity uptake nodules were observed in the hepatic hilum,
retroperitoneum, mesentery, pelvic cavity, or bilateral inguinal regions.
No fluid accumulation was noted in the abdominal and pelvic cavities.
The
physiological
curvature
of
the
spine
is
present,
with
multiple
vertebral
margins
of
the
thoracolumbar
spine
showing
osteophyte
formation.
Point-like and nodular hyperdense shadows are observed at the internal points
of the left humeral head on T4 and T12, with no increased radioactivity uptake.
Diffuse slight hyperintensity is noted in the central and proximal peripheral
bone marrow within the scan field, with an SUVmax of 3.3.
Diagnosis:
Postoperative thyroid cancer:
1. Slightly hypermetabolized flocculent shadow in the neck surgical field,
with slightly hypermetabolized soft tissue shadow anterior to the thyroid
cartilage. Combined with clinical findings, postoperative repair changes
are considered, and follow-up is recommended. A hypermetabolized mass in
the anterior segment of the left upper lobe suggests malignant lesions.
Hypermetabolized lymph nodes in the left neck, left supraclavicular fossa,
mediastinum (regions 5 and 6), and left hilum of the lung may indicate
metastatic lymph nodes. Slightly hypermetabolized lymph nodes in the right
neck (region VI) remain of undetermined nature. A hypermetabolized patchy

---

## 第 4 页

nodular shadow in the right lower lobe warrants follow-up chest CT after
anti-inflammatory treatment.
2. Possible inflammatory lymphadenopathy in the left parotid region;
recommended to be combined with ultrasonography; bulla in the upper lobe
of the left lung; slight old linear opacities in both lungs; localized
thickening of the bilateral pleura;
3. Small gallstones; increased metabolic activity at the gastric pylorus,
suggestive of inflammatory potential; uterine enlargement, possibly due to
physiological changes in both adnexa. The above findings should be
evaluated in conjunction with specialized examinations.
4.Degenerative changes in thoracolumbar spine; possible presence of T4, T12, and
left humeral head osteophytes; diffuse slight increase in metabolic activity in
the central and proximal peripheral bone marrow, suggesting possible secondary
changes. Please consider clinical evaluation.
No abnormality of metabolism was found in the neck, chest, abdomen and
pelvis.

---

## 第 1 页

PET/CT examination
report
Examination date: 2025-05-09; Tracer: 18F-FDG; Examination type: PET/CT trunk
tomography
Height: 162 cm | Weight: 59 kg | Department: Respiratory Medicine | Outpatient
Record Number
Clinical Diagnosis 1. Pulmonary Mass
Medical history and examination purpose:
The patient was diagnosed with pulmonary nodules 5 years ago and showed
no significant changes during subsequent follow-up examinations. CT scan in
2025-3 revealed a paraneoplastic mass-like shadow measuring approximately 2
cm in size, located in the left mediastinum, which was larger than before.
Medical history: The patient underwent sigmoid colon cancer surgery 5 years
ago and received multiple cycles of chemotherapy postoperatively. Regular
follow-up examinations showed no significant abnormalities. A PET/CT scan
was performed to assess the overall systemic condition.
Patient preparation and examination process:

# LC_patient_0004.pdf

## 第 1 页

18
Check Date 2023-02-21 Tracer
F-FDG Examination Item: PET/CT Trunk + Head
Tomography Height 167cm Weight 65kg Applying Department Special Chest Surgery
Clinic 2
Medical record number
Clinical Diagnosis 1: Pulmonary Mass
Medical history and purpose of examination:
In January 2023, a chest CT scan performed at another hospital revealed a left upper lung
mass with left upper lobar atelectasis and mediastinal lymphadenopathy. In February 2023, our
hospital's blood tests showed CEA at 77.1 ng/ml, NSE at 19.0 ng/ml, Cyfra211 at 8.0 ng/ml, while
SCCAg and ProGRP were within normal ranges. Medical history: 20-year asthma. A PET/CT scan
was performed to assess the nature of the lesion and overall systemic condition.
Patient preparation and examination process:
18
Fasting, establish an intravenous access, and measure blood glucose level at 5.2 mmol/L.
Administer the tracer
F-FDG intravenously (0.14-0.2 mCi/kg body weight). The patient should
remain seated and resting, with the room kept quiet, warm, and dimly lit, avoiding all external
stimuli. Prior to the examination, the patient should drink 300-500 mL of milk and urinate. Then,
the patient should lie flat and be immobilized on the examination table for trunk imaging (5 beds) +
brain imaging (1 bed), with the procedure lasting approximately 20 minutes.
Findings:
Neck: Slightly thickened mucosa of the right maxillary sinus, with no abnormal increase in
radioactivity. The nasopharyngeal, oropharyngeal, and laryngeal cavities are structurally normal, with
no local soft tissue swelling and normal distribution of radioactivity. The trachea is centrally located,
and the thyroid lobes are not enlarged with uniform density, showing no radioactivity.

---

## 第 2 页

Intravenous injection. Several radiologically enhanced lymph nodes were observed in the deep right
subclavian region (IV area) and bilateral supraclavicular regions, with the largest measuring
approximately 1.6cm in short diameter and an SUVmax of 11.1.
Thorax: An irregular soft tissue density mass with abnormally high radioactivity uptake was
observed in the left upper lobe of the lung, with indistinct demarcation from the mediastinum. The
maximum cross-sectional area was approximately 7.9×5.6 cm, with an SUVmax of 18.2. The mass
caused bronchial stenosis and obstruction in the left upper lobe of the lung, with patchy and ground-
glass opacities observed in the distal lung field, showing slightly higher radioactivity uptake, with an
SUVmax of 1.9. Multiple nodules with abnormally high radioactivity uptake were also observed in the
right middle and lower lobes of the lung, with the largest measuring 1.8×1.2cm and an SUVmax of
10.2. Multiple lymph nodes with abnormal radioactivity uptake were found in the bilateral hilar
regions and mediastinum (regions 2R,3A,3P,4R/L, 5, 6, 7), some of which showed necrosis. The
largest lymph node had a short diameter of approximately 2.8cm and an SUVmax of 15.7. The bilateral
pleura and pericardium were slightly thickened, with no abnormal radioactivity uptake observed. A
small amount of pericardial effusion was present. Several small lymph nodes with slightly increased
radioactivity were observed in the bilateral axillae, with an SUVmax of 2.2. No significant abnormal
distribution of radioactivity was noted in the chest wall.
Abdomen and Pelvis: A low-density nodule in the right lobe of the liver, with no abnormal
radiographic enhancement. The left adrenal gland is slightly thickened with mild radiographic
enhancement, showing an SUVmax of 2.9. The spleen and pancreas are normal in morphology and
size, with uniform density and normal radiographic distribution. Both kidneys are of normal size. The
right kidney exhibits multiple cystic low-density shadows, the largest measuring approximately 2.2cm
in diameter, representing areas of reduced radiolucency. The left upper pole shows a fat-like density
shadow measuring approximately 0.9×0.6 cm, with no abnormal radiographic enhancement. Minor
radiolucency is observed in the renal calyces and pelvises. The gallbladder shows no significant
tension, with smooth gallbladder and gastric walls and no abnormal radiographic distribution.
Segmental uptake is visible in some intestinal segments. The prostate is not enlarged, and nodular
calcified density shadows are present, with no clear abnormal radiographic distribution. No significant
enlargement or radiolucency is observed in the hilar region, retroperitoneum, mesentery, pelvic cavity,
or bilateral inguinal regions. No fluid accumulation is noted in the abdominal or pelvic cavities.
The physiological curvature of the vertebrae is preserved with normal sequence. Abnormal
hyperintense foci with increased radioactivity were observed at T10 and L5 vertebrae, with an
SUVmax of 13.4, and no significant bone destruction was noted. A herniated disc was present at L1/2,
with increased radioactivity at its right margin, showing an SUVmax of 5.5. Gas accumulation was
detected in the intervertebral discs of L1/2, L3/4, L4/5, and L5/S1, accompanied by partial narrowing
of the intervertebral spaces. Increased bone density was observed at the margins of multiple cervical,
thoracic, and lumbar vertebrae, with no abnormal hyperintensity on imaging.
Intracranial brain imaging revealed cortical thinning and deepened sulci, with relatively high
uptake in bilateral thalamus and basal ganglia. No significant abnormality in radioactive distribution
was observed in subcortical nuclei, cerebellum, or other regions. The morphology, size, and location
of the ventricles remained unchanged; no widening of the sulci or cisterns was noted; and midline
structures showed no displacement.
Diagnostic opinion:
1、A mass with abnormally increased metabolism in the intrinsic segment of the left upper lobe of
the lung, suggestive of malignant transformation, leading to bronchial stenosis and occlusion in the
intrinsic segment of the left upper lobe with distal obstructive changes; multiple intrapulmonary
metastases in the right middle and lower lobes of the lung; multiple lymph node metastases in the deep
right lower cervical region (region IV), bilateral supraclavicular and infraclavicular regions, bilateral
hilar regions, and mediastinum (regions 2R,3A,3P,4R/L, 5, 6, 7); bone metastases in the T10 and L5
vertebral bodies.
2、Bilateral axillary inflammatory small lymph nodes are highly probable; slight thickening of the
pleura and pericardium with minimal pericardial effusion; right maxillary sinusitis.
3、Possible right lobe cyst of the liver; slightly thickened left adrenal gland with slightly elevated
metabolism, likely physiological changes; multiple cysts in the right kidney; possible left renal pole

---

## 第 3 页

angiomysocystic lipoma; calcified lesions in the prostate.
4、L1/2 disc protrusion with hypermetabolic lesion at the right margin, suggestive of inflammatory
changes; degenerative changes in the L1-S1 intervertebral disc; degenerative changes of the spine;
5、senile brain changes;
No abnormality of metabolism was found in the head, neck, chest, abdomen and pelvis.

---

3. Specific regimen: Preferred option is pemetrexed plus cisplatin or carboplatin combined with pembrolizumab; alternatively, based on drug availability, pemetrexed plus cisplatin or carboplatin combined with other approved PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors (e.g., camrelizumab, sintilimab, tislelizumab) may be selected.
4. Subsequent management: After completion of the planned cycles, if disease progression has not occurred, maintenance therapy (pemetrexed with or without an immune checkpoint inhibitor) or observation may be considered, with regular follow-up to assess therapeutic response.
